chunks.text: Tree Implementations Chapter 16 © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Nodes in a Binary Tree • Representing tree nodes – Must contain both data and “pointers” to node’s children – Each node will be an object • Array-based – Pointers will be array indices • Link-based – Use C++ pointers © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Array-Based Representation • Class of array-based data members – Variable root is index to tree’s root node within the array tree – If tree is empty, root = -1 © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Array-Based Representation • As tree changes (additions, removals) … – Nodes may not be in contiguous array elements • Thus, need list of available nodes – Called a free list • Insert node to tree – Obtain available node from free list • Remove node from tree – Placed in free list for later use © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved
Array-Based Representation LISTING 16-1 The class TreeNode for an array-based implementation of the ADT binary tree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Array-Based Representation © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved FIGURE 16-1 (a) A binary tree of names; (b) its implementation using the array tree Is Figure 16-1 (a) a binary search tree? • yes Is Figure 16-1 (a) a balanced tree? • yes nullptr is -1
Link-Based Representation Figure 16-2 Most common way of implementing a tree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Link-Based Representation LISTING 16-2 The header file containing the class BinaryNode © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved • If the tree is empty, rootPtr contains nullptr, else it will point to the tree’s root node. • For a binary tree that is not empty, rootPtr- >getLeftChildPtr() points to the root’s left subtree, and rootPtr- >getRightChildPtr() points to the root’s right subtree. • If either of these subtrees is empty, the pointer to it would be nullptr.

The Header File LISTING 16-3 A header file for the link-based implementation of the class BinaryNodeTree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved The methods are protected (not private) so that derived classes can use them.
The Header File LISTING 16-3 A header file for the link-based implementation of the class BinaryNodeTree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

The Header File LISTING 16-3 A header file for the link-based implementation of the class BinaryNodeTree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved Public traversal methods each call a protected method that performs the actual recursion

The Implementation Constructors © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved set the value of rootPtr to nullptr sets rootPtr to point to a new node containing the given data item sets rootPtr to point to a new node containing the given data item and pointers to copies of the given subtrees

The Implementation Protected method copyTree called by copy constructor © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved To copy a tree, traverse it in preorder and insert each item visited into a new node
The Implementation destroyTree used by destructor which simply calls this method © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved To delete a tree, traverse it in postorder and delete
The Implementation Protected method getHeightHelper © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved
The Implementation Protected method that enables recursive traversals. © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Link-Based Implementation of the ADT Binary Search Tree • Uses same node objects as for binary-tree implementation. • Class BinaryNode from Listing16-2 will be used • Recursive search algorithm from Section15.3.2 is basis for operations © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Link-Based Implementation of the ADT Binary Search Tree FIGURE 16-7 Adding Kody to a binary search tree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved • If you take the output of a preorder traversal of a binary search tree and use it with insertInorder to create a binary search tree, you will obtain a duplicate tree. Tree (a) was used in previous chapter. • The copy constructor for the ADT binary tree used a preorder traversal to copy the tree. • To insert Kody, search the BST, and search algorithm would terminate at Mia’s left child
Link-Based Implementation of the ADT Binary Search Tree Figure 16-8 Adding new data to a binary search tree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Link-Based Implementation of the ADT Binary Search Tree 3 cases for removing node N: 1. N is a leaf o Remove leaf containing target o Set pointer in parent to nullptr 2. N has only left (or right) child – cases are symmetrical o After N removed, all data items rooted at L (or R) are adopted by root of N o All items adopted are in correct order, binary search tree property preserved 3. N has two children o Locate another node M easier to remove from tree than N o Copy item that is in M to N o Remove M from tree © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Link-Based Implementation of the ADT Binary Search Tree Figure 16-9 Case 2 for removeValue: The data item to remove is in a node N that has only a left child and whose parent is node P © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Link-Based Implementation of the ADT Binary Search Tree FIGURE16-11 Not any node will do © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Link-Based Implementation of the ADT Binary Search Tree FIGURE 16-13 Replacing the data item in node N with its inorder successor © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved • inorder successor of a data item is the leftmost node of N’s right subtree.

Saving a Binary Search Tree in a File FIGURE 16-15 An initially empty binary search tree after the addition of 60, 20, 10, 40, 30, 50, and 70 © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Saving a Binary Search Tree in a File • Use preorder traversal to save binary search tree in a file – Restore to original shape by using method add • Balanced binary search tree increases efficiency of ADT operations © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved
Saving a Binary Search Tree in a File FIGURE 16-16 A full tree saved in a file by using inorder traversal © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved
Saving a Binary Search Tree in a File Building a minimum-height binary search tree by reading sorted data sequentially from file © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved

Tree Sort Tree sort uses a binary search tree. © 2017 Pearson Education, Hoboken, NJ. All rights reserved • Tree sort: • Insertion is O(log n) in the average case, and O(n) in the worse case • Thus, insertion is O(n × log n) in the average case, and O(n2) in the worst case • Traversal involves one copy: O(n) • Thus, traversal is O(n × log n) in the average case, and O(n2) in the worst case
